question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TransactionRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a minimum-rows-remaining guard in prune() to cause the RetentionJob to delete the last transaction in the database, leaving the importer with no anchor point for the next retention cycle and causing it to skip all future pruning or crash on 30%+ of nodes?
